items.briefing:
A widely used JavaScript security tool called Jscrambler has been compromised — hackers inserted malware into its npm software package that can steal information from affected systems. If your organisation uses JavaScript development tools or has a software development team, check with your IT provider to ensure this package has not been used in any systems or websites.

A local study by CyberPath has also highlighted that many Australians face significant barriers when trying to enter the cybersecurity workforce, which may affect how organisations find and retain security talent going forward.

**Tip:** Ask your IT team or provider to regularly audit any third-party software packages used in your systems — compromised tools can introduce threats without obvious warning signs.

featured.title: CyberPath study says barriers block cyber job entry
featured.quote: Certification barriers prevent Australians from entering high-demand cybersecurity careers.
